proficiencyScale.label: Awareness
proficiencyScale.description: Knows the concept exists; can describe it at a high level

proficiencyScale.label: Foundational
proficiencyScale.description: Can execute with guidance; has applied it in limited scope

proficiencyScale.label: Practitioner
proficiencyScale.description: Works independently; applies in real environments regularly

proficiencyScale.label: Advanced
proficiencyScale.description: Deep working knowledge; can design, troubleshoot, and guide others

proficiencyScale.label: Expert
proficiencyScale.description: Authoritative; designs systems, sets standards, mentors others at this skill

skills.label: Microsoft Entra ID
masteryLevels: Can describe what Entra ID is and explain the difference between cloud-only and hybrid identity models to a colleague.
Creates users and groups, assigns RBAC roles, and configures a basic Conditional Access policy in a test tenant following documentation.
Manages Managed Identities for Azure workloads, configures SSO for SaaS applications, and enforces MFA via Conditional Access policies in production.
Designs multi-tenant identity architectures, implements Privileged Identity Management for just-in-time access, and diagnoses complex token flows and claims-mapping failures.
Defines the organisation's identity governance strategy; architects cross-tenant, B2B/B2C, and hybrid AD DS integration patterns; advises engineering teams on regulatory compliance requirements.

resources.title: Implement Microsoft Entra identity management — MS Learn path

skills.label: Azure Key Vault
masteryLevels: Can explain what Azure Key Vault is, describe the three object types it manages (secrets, keys, and certificates), and articulate why centralising credentials in Key Vault is preferable to application config files or environment variables.

Creates a Key Vault, stores a secret, and retrieves it from an application using the Azure SDK authenticated with a managed identity, configuring an access policy that grants the identity read access to secrets only.

Migrates application configurations from hardcoded values to Key Vault references in App Service and Azure Functions, switches from legacy access policies to RBAC-based access control, and restricts Key Vault to a private endpoint to eliminate public exposure.

Designs a Key Vault architecture for a multi-application environment with environment-separated vaults, implements automated certificate lifecycle management with auto-rotation, and configures diagnostic logging with alerts on unauthorised access attempts or policy changes.

Defines the organisation's secrets management strategy using Key Vault; establishes vault segmentation, RBAC, and key rotation policies as organisational standards; audits compliance across all subscriptions; advises on Key Vault integration patterns for Bicep IaC, CI/CD pipelines, and AKS workload identity.

resources.title: Configure and manage Azure Key Vault — MS Learn

skills.label: Azure Policy
masteryLevels: Can explain what Azure Policy is, describe how it differs from RBAC (controlling what resources look like vs. what actions you can perform), and identify the key effect types: Audit, Deny, Append, Modify, and DeployIfNotExists.

Assigns a built-in policy definition to a resource group, reviews the compliance report to identify non-compliant resources, and interprets why a specific resource is flagged against the assigned definition.

Authors a custom policy definition in JSON, groups related policies into an initiative, assigns the initiative at subscription scope, and creates a remediation task to bring existing non-compliant resources into compliance using DeployIfNotExists.

Implements Policy-as-Code by managing definitions and assignments in a Git repository with CI/CD deployment, designs an initiative structure enforcing tagging standards, allowed locations, and SKU compliance across all subscriptions, and uses compliance data to produce governance posture reports.

Defines the organisation's cloud governance framework using Azure Policy; architects the initiative hierarchy across management groups; establishes the policy lifecycle from authoring through testing to enforcement mode; presents compliance posture trends to audit and security leadership.

resources.title: Build a cloud governance strategy on Azure — MS Learn

skills.label: Microsoft Defender for Cloud
masteryLevels: Can describe what Microsoft Defender for Cloud is, explain the difference between CSPM (security posture management) and CWPP (workload protection), and identify the distinction between free foundational coverage and paid Defender plans.
Enables Defender for Cloud on a subscription, reviews the Secure Score dashboard, and identifies the top recommendations that offer the greatest posture improvement for the least remediation effort.

Enables Defender plans for specific workload types (Servers, Databases, Storage), investigates and remediates security recommendations, configures regulatory compliance dashboards against a framework such as CIS or NIST SP 800-53, and sets up email alerts for high-severity threats.

Designs a Defender for Cloud deployment across a multi-subscription estate, implements auto-provisioning for monitoring agents and extensions, integrates security alerts with Microsoft Sentinel, and connects source code repositories to enable DevSecOps posture management.

Defines the organisation's cloud security posture management strategy; owns the Secure Score improvement roadmap and assigns remediation ownership to subscription teams; advises on Defender plan selection based on workload risk and cost; presents regulatory compliance posture to audit committees.

resources.title: Defender for Cloud security recommendations reference
Mitigate threats with Microsoft Defender for Cloud — MS Learn path

skills.label: Azure Blueprints / Landing Zones
skills.notes: Azure Blueprints deprecated July 2026 — Landing Zone architecture via CAF is the durable skill
masteryLevels: Can describe the components of an Azure Landing Zone — management groups, subscriptions, policies, and shared platform services — and explain why a structured landing zone accelerates secure, governed cloud adoption at scale.

Deploys a basic management group hierarchy (Platform, Landing Zones, Sandboxes) and assigns built-in policies and RBAC at management group scope, demonstrating how settings inherit to all child subscriptions.

Implements a Landing Zone architecture following the Cloud Adoption Framework, configuring connectivity, identity, and management subscriptions; understands that Azure Blueprints is deprecated in favour of Bicep and Deployment Stacks for landing zone IaC delivery.

Designs and deploys a multi-subscription Landing Zone using Bicep and Deployment Stacks, configures policy inheritance to enforce compliance guardrails across all child subscriptions, and establishes a subscription vending process for self-service workload onboarding.

Defines the organisation's landing zone architecture and governance strategy; leads the platform team that owns connectivity, identity, and management subscriptions; evaluates the Azure Landing Zone accelerator; manages the platform's evolution as the organisation scales to many subscriptions.

resources.title: Azure Landing Zones documentation — Cloud Adoption Framework

skills.label: Zero Trust Architecture
masteryLevels: Can describe the Zero Trust principle of 'never trust, always verify' and contrast it with traditional perimeter-based security to a non-technical audience.
Maps an organisation's existing controls against the NIST Zero Trust Architecture pillars and produces a gap analysis identifying the highest-priority remediation actions.

Implements Conditional Access policies, enforces least-privilege access via PIM and RBAC, and configures network micro-segmentation using NSGs and Private Endpoints to eliminate implicit trust from workloads.

Designs a Zero Trust architecture spanning identity, devices, applications, network, and data planes; integrates telemetry across all planes into a SIEM for continuous validation and automated response.

Leads an organisation's Zero Trust transformation programme; evaluates vendor solutions across all pillars, defines the multi-year target-state architecture, and presents strategy and measurable progress to executive stakeholders.

skills.label: Virtual Network (VNet)
masteryLevels: Can describe what an Azure VNet is, explain the relationship between address spaces and subnets, and identify why VNet isolation is the foundation of network security in Azure.

Creates a VNet with multiple subnets using Bicep, assigns address ranges that avoid overlap with on-premises or other VNets, and enables Service Endpoints on a subnet to provide a network path to specific Azure services.

Configures VNet Peering for direct routing between two VNets, designs a Hub-Spoke topology where a hub VNet hosts shared services (firewall, DNS, VPN gateway), and understands the routing implications of peering including the absence of transitive routing without a hub.

Designs a full landing zone VNet architecture with dedicated subnets for each tier (management, application, data, gateway), implements NSG and route table associations at the subnet level, and architects the peering model for spoke VNets to reach the internet and on-premises via the hub.

Defines the organisation's VNet addressing scheme and subnet design standards; governs VNet creation, address allocation, and peering through Bicep and Azure Policy; advises on Hub-Spoke versus Virtual WAN topology decisions; leads the network design for enterprise-scale landing zones.

skills.label: Azure Firewall
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure Firewall is, explain how it differs from NSGs (stateful L7 service with FQDN filtering versus stateful L4 port rules), and identify the three rule collection types: DNAT, Network, and Application.
Deploys Azure Firewall Standard in a hub VNet, creates network rule collections to allow specific traffic flows, and configures a DNAT rule to forward inbound internet traffic to a private backend.

Manages Azure Firewall using Firewall Policy, writes application rule collections to allow FQDN-based outbound internet access for spoke workloads, enables Threat Intelligence filtering to block traffic to known malicious destinations, and routes spoke subnet traffic through the firewall using User Defined Routes.

Deploys Azure Firewall Premium with IDPS in Alert and Deny mode for deep packet inspection, enables TLS inspection for encrypted traffic analysis, and designs a parent-child Firewall Policy hierarchy so organisation-wide baseline rules are inherited by all deployment-specific policies.

Defines the organisation's network security perimeter strategy using Azure Firewall; designs the policy hierarchy and rule governance process; evaluates Azure Firewall versus third-party NVAs for specific inspection requirements; leads the network security model design for the Azure landing zone.

resources.title: Introduction to Azure Firewall — MS Learn

skills.label: Azure Application Gateway
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure Application Gateway is, explain its role as a regional L7 load balancer, and distinguish it from Azure Load Balancer (L4) and Azure Front Door (global CDN and WAF).

Deploys an Application Gateway with a basic HTTP listener and backend pool, routes traffic to App Service or VM backends, and validates that health probes correctly mark backends healthy or unhealthy.

Configures URL path-based routing rules to direct traffic to different backend pools, enables the WAF v2 SKU with the OWASP managed ruleset in Detection mode, and terminates SSL at the gateway using a certificate stored in Key Vault.

Enables WAF Prevention mode after validating no legitimate traffic is blocked, creates custom WAF rules to handle application-specific patterns, configures autoscaling to handle variable traffic, and implements a private frontend IP for internal application load balancing.

Defines the organisation's regional web application ingress strategy; evaluates Application Gateway versus Front Door versus third-party WAF solutions for different geographic and performance requirements; governs WAF ruleset management and exclusion justification processes; leads WAF tuning for production applications.

resources.title: Load balance HTTP traffic with Application Gateway — MS Learn

skills.label: Azure Front Door
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure Front Door is, explain its global anycast routing capabilities, and identify when Front Door is appropriate versus Application Gateway — global multi-region delivery versus single-region L7 load balancing.
Creates a Front Door profile, adds an origin group pointing to an App Service or storage account, configures health probes, and maps a custom domain with an auto-provisioned managed TLS certificate.

Configures URL path-based routing rules to direct traffic to different origins, attaches WAF policies with OWASP managed rules to protect against web attacks, and uses response caching rules to reduce origin load for static and semi-static content.

Designs a global application delivery architecture with Front Door routing across multiple Azure regions for active-active or active-passive failover, implements geo-filtering to restrict access by country, and configures origin groups with weighted load balancing for blue-green deployments.

Defines the organisation's global application delivery strategy; evaluates Front Door Standard versus Premium and Azure CDN profiles against content and security requirements; designs the origin topology and routing rules for multi-region applications; governs WAF policy lifecycle and exclusion approval processes.

resources.title: Introduction to Azure Front Door — MS Learn

skills.label: Private Link / Private Endpoints
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure Private Link and Private Endpoints are, explain how they bring PaaS services into a VNet's private address space, and identify why they are critical for preventing data exfiltration and meeting compliance requirements.
Creates a Private Endpoint for an Azure Storage account, verifies it receives a private IP in the target subnet, and tests that the storage account is reachable from a VM in the same VNet.

Configures a Private DNS Zone linked to the VNet so the PaaS service's hostname resolves to the private IP instead of the public endpoint, applies NSG rules to control which subnets can reach the Private Endpoint, and disables the PaaS service's public network access to complete the isolation.

Designs a private connectivity architecture for a Hub-Spoke topology where Private Endpoints in a central spoke or hub DNS landing zone are accessible from all other spokes via DNS forwarding, and deploys Private DNS Zone integration at scale using Azure Policy with DINE (DeployIfNotExists) effects.

Defines the organisation's private connectivity standards; mandates Private Endpoints for all PaaS services handling sensitive data through Azure Policy; architects the Private DNS Zone hierarchy for consistent name resolution; advises on Private Link Service design for exposing internal services to customers or partners.

resources.title: Introduction to Azure Private Link — MS Learn

skills.label: VPN Gateway / ExpressRoute
masteryLevels: Can describe the three Azure hybrid connectivity options (Site-to-Site VPN, Point-to-Site VPN, ExpressRoute) and identify when each is appropriate based on bandwidth, latency, reliability, and cost requirements.
Deploys an Azure VPN Gateway and configures a Site-to-Site IPsec tunnel to an on-premises VPN device, verifies connectivity by routing test traffic between Azure and on-premises address ranges.

Configures a Point-to-Site VPN for remote user access using Azure AD authentication, implements BGP on a Site-to-Site VPN for dynamic route exchange, and designs the address space to prevent IP overlap between Azure VNets and on-premises networks.

Configures ExpressRoute with a provider circuit, implements ExpressRoute FastPath for low-latency workloads, and deploys ExpressRoute and VPN Gateway in coexistence on the same VNet for failover resilience when the private circuit is unavailable.

Defines the organisation's hybrid connectivity strategy; evaluates Site-to-Site VPN versus ExpressRoute versus Virtual WAN for bandwidth, latency, and cost requirements; designs the connectivity model for multiple offices and regions; leads the circuit ordering and peering configuration with network and carrier teams.

resources.title: Connect your on-premises network to Azure with VPN — MS Learn

skills.label: Azure DNS
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure DNS is, explain the difference between Public zones (internet-resolvable) and Private zones (VNet-internal resolution), and identify why Azure-managed DNS removes the operational burden of running DNS server infrastructure.
Creates a Public DNS zone, adds A, CNAME, and TXT records, delegates the zone from a domain registrar to Azure DNS name servers, and verifies resolution using nslookup or dig from an external client.

Creates a Private DNS Zone, links it to a VNet for VM auto-registration, configures the zone for Private Endpoint integration so PaaS services resolve to their private IPs, and implements split-horizon DNS so the same domain resolves differently inside and outside Azure.

Deploys Azure DNS Private Resolver with inbound and outbound endpoints to forward queries bidirectionally between on-premises DNS and Azure Private DNS, designs conditional forwarding rules so on-premises clients can resolve Azure private DNS names without relying on custom DNS servers in Azure VMs.

Defines the organisation's DNS architecture; designs the zone hierarchy, Private Resolver topology, and forwarding ruleset for a full hybrid environment; governs DNS zone creation, record TTLs, and Private Endpoint DNS integration through Bicep and policy; advises on DNS root authority and delegation decisions.

resources.title: Host a domain on Azure DNS — MS Learn

skills.label: Azure DDoS Protection
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure DDoS Protection is, explain the difference between the default Basic protection (included with all Azure subscriptions) and the paid Network Protection plan, and identify the attack types each tier addresses.

Enables Azure DDoS Network Protection on a VNet, reviews the per-public-IP protection policies automatically tuned by the service, and understands the telemetry available in Azure Monitor during and after a DDoS event.

Exports DDoS diagnostic logs to a Log Analytics workspace for attack event analysis, creates metric-based alerts to notify the operations team when an attack is detected or mitigated, and validates that all internet-facing public IP resources in scope have protection coverage.

Designs a DDoS protection architecture covering all internet-facing public IPs across the estate, registers for Microsoft's DDoS Rapid Response team access for expert assistance during active attacks, and integrates DDoS telemetry with a SIEM for correlation with other security events.

Defines the organisation's DDoS protection strategy; evaluates Network Protection versus IP Protection tiers against cost and coverage requirements per workload; establishes the incident response playbook for DDoS events; presents DDoS risk posture and mitigation effectiveness to security leadership.

Introduction to Azure DDoS Protection — MS Learn

skills.label: Network Security Groups (NSG)
masteryLevels: Can describe what a Network Security Group is, explain how priority-ordered inbound and outbound rules determine whether traffic is allowed or denied, and identify the default rules Azure adds to every NSG.

Creates an NSG, adds inbound allow rules for specific ports and source ranges, associates it with a subnet, and uses Network Watcher IP flow verify to confirm traffic is permitted or blocked as expected.

Implements Application Security Groups to group VMs by application tier and write rules that reference ASGs instead of IP ranges, making the ruleset maintainable as IPs change; enables NSG Flow Logs to capture traffic metadata for security investigation.

Designs an NSG strategy for a landing zone with deny-all defaults and explicit allow rules at both subnet and NIC levels, integrates Flow Logs with Traffic Analytics to visualise network patterns and identify overly permissive rules, and enforces NSG requirements on all subnets at creation using Azure Policy.

Defines the organisation's NSG baseline ruleset and design standards; governs NSG rule creation, justification, and review cadence through Bicep templates and Azure Policy; uses Traffic Analytics to drive least-privilege remediation; trains engineering teams on network security group design principles.

resources.title: Filter network traffic with NSGs — MS Learn

skills.label: Azure Load Balancer
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure Load Balancer is, explain the difference between Public and Internal (private) load balancers, and identify the key distinctions between Basic and Standard SKUs.

Creates a Standard Public Load Balancer, defines a backend pool from a set of VMs, configures a TCP health probe on the application port, and verifies that traffic distributes across healthy instances.

Configures load-balancing rules with session persistence for stateful applications, adds inbound NAT rules for direct management access to individual backend instances, and deploys an Internal Load Balancer to distribute traffic between application tiers within a VNet.

Designs a zone-redundant Load Balancer architecture using Standard SKU with cross-zone load balancing, integrates it with VMSS for elastic backend pools, and implements floating IP to support SQL Server Always On Availability Group listeners without requiring listener IPs on each VM.

Defines the organisation's load balancing architecture standards; evaluates Azure Load Balancer versus Application Gateway versus Front Door for L4 versus L7 versus global traffic patterns; designs health probe strategies for reliable traffic routing; governs Load Balancer deployment through Bicep and naming standards.

skills.label: Azure NAT Gateway
masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure NAT Gateway is, explain why it solves the SNAT port exhaustion problem that affects VMs making high volumes of outbound connections, and identify when NAT Gateway is the right outbound connectivity solution.
Deploys a NAT Gateway with a public IP address, associates it with a subnet, and confirms that outbound connections from VMs use the NAT Gateway's IP rather than the default Azure outbound SNAT.

Configures NAT Gateway with a public IP prefix to provide a predictable range of outbound source IPs for allowlisting at external services, and associates the gateway with zone-redundant resources for resilient outbound connectivity.

Designs an outbound connectivity architecture that routes all production subnet internet traffic through NAT Gateway for consistent and reliable SNAT, eliminates default outbound access ahead of Azure's retirement of it, and shares NAT Gateway public IPs across spoke subnets in a Hub-Spoke topology via routing.

Defines the organisation's outbound internet connectivity standards; mandates NAT Gateway for all production subnets through Azure Policy; designs the public IP prefix strategy for subnets requiring predictable source IPs; advises on the NAT Gateway versus Azure Firewall decision for subnets with different outbound inspection requirements.

Deploys a VMSS using a Bicep template, uses a Custom Script Extension to install software on instances at boot, and manually adjusts the instance count to validate scaling behaviour.

skills.label: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
masteryLevels: Can describe what AKS is, explain how it manages the Kubernetes control plane on your behalf, and identify the key concepts: node pools, the API server, kubelet, and how kubectl issues commands to the cluster.

Deploys a containerised application to a Container Apps environment, exposes it on a public ingress endpoint, and configures minimum replicas and HTTP scaling rules to handle variable traffic.

Configures Scale-to-Zero for event-driven workloads to eliminate idle cost, enables the Dapr sidecar for service discovery and pub/sub messaging between container apps, and provisions a managed TLS certificate for a custom domain.

Designs a Container Apps environment with internal VNet-integrated ingress for private workloads, implements multi-container apps with Dapr components for state and pub/sub, and deploys Container Apps via GitHub Actions CI/CD with blue-green revision switching and traffic splitting.

masteryLevels: Can describe what Azure Container Instances is, explain when it is appropriate (fast, serverless container execution) versus AKS or Container Apps (long-running, orchestrated workloads), and identify the billing model.
